author: @ThisRandomUsername
text:
I can't tell very well how it came out, but if you use a vector image instead of a raster (pixellated), it will likely give you a cleaner cut. Most drawing programs will have a tool that lets you convert raster images quite easily. In Inkscape, it's Path -> Trace Bitmap. You can then export as a DWG or SVG or something your laser cutter supports. Make sure it's the lines you're exporting, not the background image.

Another thing: Why do you need to make it into a png if you just export it as a jpeg again afterwards?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Thanks for the tip! The reason why I exported it to jpeg is because of our laser cutting program. It is the only way I know how to make it work lol. Thanks mate!
